{{Chess Opening Theory/Position|= |Symmetrical English| |rd| |bd|qd|kd| |nd|rd|= |pd|pd| |pd| |pd|bd|pd|= | | |nd| | | |pd| |= | | |pd| |pd| | | |= | | |pl| | | | | |= | | |nl| | |nl|pl| |= |pl|pl| |pl|pl|pl|bl|pl|= |rl| |bl|ql|kl| | |rl|= || }} = Symmetrical English - Wedberg Variation= ===5...e5=== Named after Tom Wedberg, who developed and popularized in the 80's the idea of 5...e5 followed by Nge7. ==Theory table== {{Chess Opening Theory/Table}}. '''1.c4 c5 2.Nc3 Nc6 3.g3 g6 4.Bg2 Bg7 5.Nf3 e5'''
6 7 8 9
1 [[/6. O-O|O-O]]
Nge7		 a3
O-O		 Rb1
 
2 [[/6. a3|a3]]
a5		 d4!?
cxd4		 Nb5
d6		 e3
 
3 d3
Nge7		 Nd2
d6		 Nf1
 
(with the idea of Ne3)
 
4 Rb1


(Usually transpose to the other lines)
